title: LABEL-seq Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1loi8","text":"Pre-barcoded Vector Design:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"82ud0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qt0e","text":"This protocol outlines the materials and methods for conducting a LABEL-seq (Labeling with Barcodes and Enrichment for Biochemical Analysis by Sequencing) assay. The assay utilizes designated plasmids containing Bxb1-attB sites and MS2 phage coat protein (MCP) binding elements that link with small MS2 RNA hairpins. A gene of interest (GOI) can be fused to MCP either at the N-terminus (N-term) or C-terminus (C-term), enabling the expression of stable circular RNAs (circRNAs) that incorporate MS2 hairpins along with a unique 18-nucleotide barcode. Each protein variant is tagged with a specific barcode, allowing quantitative measurement of gene activity and protein abundance. The current (as of 2025) working system (LABEL-seq v2) is modified based on the published version (Simon et al. 2024). There are two major modifications made for a two-step cloning system compatible with Golden Gate Assembly. First, a pair of BsmBi/ Esp3I recognition sequences, located on the circRNA architecture, is used to add barcode sequences to the vector. Secondly, a pair of SapI recognition sequences were added to assist cloning of the GOI variant library.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
